---
title: "Raphael Ludwig"
format:
  typst:
    papersize: a4
    margin:
      x: 1.8cm
      y: 1.6cm
    fontsize: 10pt
    mainfont: "Georgia"
execute:
  echo: false
---

<!--
TODO (Raphael): review this English translation of the original Portuguese CV before
sharing. Phone and home address are intentionally omitted from this public-download
version — add them back here if you want them on the PDF.
-->

Quantitative Economist \| raphaludwig@gmail.com \| linkedin.com/in/raphaludwig <!-- TODO: confirm LinkedIn URL -->

## Experience

**Economist — Vista Capital**, Dec 2020 – May 2026

- Structured the Macro Research area from inception with the chief economist — processes,
  analytical routines, data infrastructure, and research products used daily by the fund.
- Built a proprietary macroeconomic data platform (Docker on Linux, later migrated to
  Prefect) with autonomous monitoring, alerts, and automatic reports; maintained 250+
  internal data routines.
- Developed and coordinated macro projection models, including a proprietary
  model-selection algorithm and an automated short/medium-term forecasting system.
- Replicated and adapted empirical exercises from central banks, academic papers, and
  sell-side research; built internal AI-based automation tools (Claude Code).
- Led Brazil and Europe (Euro Area, UK) macro coverage — data, scenario analysis,
  meetings with policy actors — plus thematic coverage (e.g., Covid-19, supply chains).
- Trained intern cohorts in programming and macro topics; led internal trainings on
  economics, automation, and applied AI.

## Education

**MSc in Economics — PUC-Rio**, Jan 2019 – Jul 2021
Dissertation: *"On the Missing Disinflation Puzzle: A Data-Driven Approach"* (advisors:
E. Zilberman, M. Medeiros).

**BSc in Economics — PUC-Rio**, Jan 2013 – Jul 2017
PET/PIBIC research programs (forecasting, machine learning); TA for Programming
Techniques for Economics II. Thesis: *"Nowcasting Unemployment with Google Trends:
Evidence from the Brazilian Labor Market"*.

## Skills

**Quant macro research** — forecasting, nowcasting, applied econometrics, machine
learning, scenario analysis.
**Programming & data** — R, Python, MATLAB/Octave, Dynare, Git.
**Infrastructure** — Docker, Linux/VMs, Prefect, Airflow, Shiny, Streamlit, JavaScript.

## Languages

Portuguese (native) · English (advanced) · German (advanced) · Spanish (basic)
